option.name: title_text
option.value: Nursing Leadership Strategies for Success - MOD 1

string_table.name: npnxnanbnsnfns00001101101
string_table.string: To watch this video, use the preview feature in Storyline 360, upload your published output to a server, publish to Review 360, or switch to static video quality.
Streaming video is unavailable for local playback.
Use the w, a, s, and d keys to move around the 360 degree image. Press the tab key to jump to interactive markers and hotspots.
You must complete the question before submitting.
Enter your name in the field below:
You have reached the time limit set for the quiz. Press 'OK' to continue.

slidelink.displaytext: Nursing Leadership Strategies for Success - MOD 1
Do you have an RN or LPN license issued by the Alabama Board of Nursing?
The way to get started is to quit talking and begin doing.
According to Richard Branson, what is the key to ensuring employees stay with an organization?
Which of the following is a primary responsibility of administrators and executives in an organization?
What role does the nursing administrator play in helping the organization fulfill its mission and reach its goals?
What is a key distinction between the role of a nurse administrator and a nurse manager?
What is one of the key responsibilities of a nurse administrator in empowering and informing staff?

resource_data.description: Here are some useful links and documents:

slidetranscripts.slidetranscript: <Document xmlns:xsd="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance"> <Content> <Block> <Style /> <Span Text="Administrators and executives establish an organization’s mission, vision, values, and goals. In this course, consisting of three modules, you will be provided information and tools to help you establish a foundation for leading an organization into the future." /> </Block> </Content> </Document>

<Document xmlns:xsd="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance"> <Content> <Block> <Style /> <Span Text="Upon completion of Module 1, the participant should be able to:
" /> </Block> <Block> <Style /> <Span Text="·	Describe the first of three strategies that contribute to organizational success: influences, goals, & infrastructure.
" /> </Block> <Block> <Style /> <Span Text="·	Identify influences that impact organizational success." /> </Block> </Content> </Document>

<Document xmlns:xsd="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance"> <Content> <Block> <Style /> <Span Text="AUDIO REDO: For purposes of this course, the title Nurse Administrator will be used to represent the highest-ranking nurse or nurses in an organization and the role that is responsible for the overall provision of nursing services within a facility or across an organization.
" /> </Block> <Block> <Style /> <Span Text="
" /> </Block> <Block> <Style /> </Block> </Content> </Document>

<Document xmlns:xsd="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance"> <Content> <Block> <Style /> <Span Text="First, it is crucial that the nurse administrator recognizes that he or she is not a manager. Management tasks include those necessary for the day-to-day operation of individual services, units, and departments. Second, the nurse administrator must ensure that directors and managers are informed of the organization’s plan to meet established goals and initiatives, especially during times of change within the organization. Third, he or she must empower directors and managers to carry out these functions as envisioned by the organization’s administration." /> </Block> </Content> </Document>

<Document xmlns:xsd="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance"> <Content> <Block> <Style /> <Span Text="In some smaller organizations, it isn’t unusual for nurses to advance from staff to manager to administrator in a relatively rapid sequence, and training for expectations at each level may be minimal or even non-existent. This is why the ABN developed its Leadership Institute - to train nurses in various leadership concepts, like effective communication, conflict resolution, nursing regulation, collaboration, and critical thinking." /> </Block> </Content> </Document>

<Document xmlns:xsd="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance"> <Content> <Block> <Style FlowDirection="LeftToRight" LeadingMargin="0" TrailingMargin="0" FirstLineMargin="0" Justification="Left" LineSpacingRule="Multiple" LineSpacing="21" SpacingBefore="0" SpacingAfter="8"> <ListStyle ListType="None" ListTypeFormat="Parentheses" Color="#000000" BulletFont="Arial" /> </Style> <Span Text="Nurse administrators are similar to super heroes. Consider this: There are very few of nurses who aspire to the top-level nursing position in an organization, so congratulations if you are among the small number of nurses who have achieved that status. However, if you either have not yet reached this goal, or even if you have no desire to do so, this course will help you develop the global scale thinking that is essential to the nurse administrator role.
" /> </Block> <Block> <Style FlowDirection="LeftToRight" LeadingMargin="0" TrailingMargin="0" FirstLineMargin="0" Justification="Left" LineSpacingRule="Multiple" LineSpacing="21" SpacingBefore="0" SpacingAfter="8"> <ListStyle ListType="None" ListTypeFormat="Parentheses" Color="#000000" BulletFont="Arial" /> </Style> </Block> </Content> </Document>

<Document xmlns:xsd="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance"> <Content> <Block> <Style FlowDirection="LeftToRight" LeadingMargin="0" TrailingMargin="0" FirstLineMargin="0" Justification="Left" LineSpacingRule="Multiple" LineSpacing="21" SpacingBefore="0" SpacingAfter="8"> <ListStyle ListType="None" ListTypeFormat="Parentheses" BulletFont="Arial" /> </Style> <Span Text="The first strategy for success is to evaluate the varied internal and external influences that affect the organization’s plan to achieve goals. Nurse administrators are integral to the achievement of organizational goals because they serve at the crossroads of policy and implementation. Notably, these concepts impact nurses at every level - even staff nurses must educate and explain the need to do something in a certain way based on standards. This becomes increasingly complex with each advancement up the ladder of leadership. Again, this course is intended to contribute to your knowledge to fill the gap and positively contribute to your ability to impact and influence others. " /> </Block> </Content> </Document>

<Document xmlns:xsd="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance"> <Content> <Block> <Style FlowDirection="LeftToRight" LeadingMargin="0" TrailingMargin="0" FirstLineMargin="0" Justification="Left" LineSpacingRule="Multiple" LineSpacing="21" SpacingBefore="0" SpacingAfter="8"> <ListStyle ListType="None" ListTypeFormat="Parentheses" BulletFont="Arial" /> </Style> <Span Text="External influences may have more impact on an organization than internal influences. When you consider that the media is filled with stories, accounts, and influencers that offer opinions on everything from compensation issues to violence in the healthcare workplace, the savvy nurse administrator must monitor current events. It is impossible for a single nurse leader to be current on every topic that may interest their staff, so it is important to develop and maintain a professional leadership network and to make currency in nursing and healthcare-focused literature a routine pursuit." /> </Block> </Content> </Document>

<Document xmlns:xsd="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance"> <Content> <Block> <Style FlowDirection="LeftToRight" LeadingMargin="0" TrailingMargin="0" FirstLineMargin="0" Justification="Left" LineSpacingRule="Multiple" LineSpacing="21" SpacingBefore="0" SpacingAfter="8"> <ListStyle ListType="None" ListTypeFormat="Parentheses" BulletFont="Arial" /> </Style> <Span Text="Additional external influences the nurse administrator has to consider are often financial and may even be political. For example, if a local politician might hold a press conference and demand that the organization step up its screenings for diabetes and cholesterol, or CMS could impose new standards for documentation to ensure maximum reimbursement, understanding and confronting these issues is critical to your success." /> </Block> </Content> </Document>

<Document xmlns:xsd="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance"> <Content> <Block> <Style FlowDirection="LeftToRight" LeadingMargin="0" TrailingMargin="0" FirstLineMargin="0" Justification="Left" LineSpacingRule="Multiple" LineSpacing="21" SpacingBefore="0" SpacingAfter="8"> <ListStyle ListType="None" ListTypeFormat="Parentheses" Color="#000000" BulletFont="Arial" /> </Style> <Span Text="Balancing all of the influences to meet needs is the duty of the nurse administrator. Employees will feel dissatisfied if they can’t provide a basic service like giving a patient a bed bath, especially when that action may be contributing to the development of a post-operative infection. 
" /> </Block> <Block> <Style FlowDirection="LeftToRight" LeadingMargin="0" TrailingMargin="0" FirstLineMargin="0" Justification="Left" LineSpacingRule="Multiple" LineSpacing="21" SpacingBefore="0" SpacingAfter="8"> <ListStyle ListType="None" ListTypeFormat="Parentheses" Color="#000000" BulletFont="Arial" /> </Style> <Span Text="So, what happened in that situation?
" /> </Block> <Block> <Style FlowDirection="LeftToRight" LeadingMargin="0" TrailingMargin="0" FirstLineMargin="0" Justification="Left" LineSpacingRule="Multiple" LineSpacing="21" SpacingBefore="0" SpacingAfter="8"> <ListStyle ListType="None" ListTypeFormat="Parentheses" BulletFont="Arial" /> </Style> <Span Text="The nurse administrator listened and took appropriate action. The priority was the patients and infections. Therefore, to address the current infections and the concerns about staff utilizing the sinks for baths. The nurse administrator called the Infection Control Nurse and asked that all sinks be immediately cultured in both critical units. The test results indicated that the sinks were the source of the infection. Staff were given immediate education and retraining related to correct bathing procedures and sources of infection. In addition, all sinks were treated for bacterial contaminants. The current infections were treated and there were no new infections. The nurse administrator also reviewed the original plans, which made it clear that the sink size was changed during construction. Although she knew that getting this situation remedied was going to cause grief for the construction team and the nurses who worked on the unit, the temporary inconvenience was important for patient safety, staff satisfaction, and ultimately, the long-term success of the organization. The sink issue had to be corrected going forward. " /> </Block> </Content> </Document>

<Document xmlns:xsd="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance"> <Content> <Block> <Style FlowDirection="LeftToRight" LeadingMargin="0" TrailingMargin="0" FirstLineMargin="0" Justification="Left" LineSpacingRule="Multiple" LineSpacing="21" SpacingBefore="0" SpacingAfter="8"> <ListStyle ListType="None" ListTypeFormat="Parentheses" BulletFont="Arial" /> </Style> <Span Text="It might sound simplistic, but happy employees take care of your customers. In healthcare, those customers are primarily the patients and their families. There are many successful business leaders who focus on employees to meet organizational objectives. One of these is Richard Branson, entrepreneur and founder of the Virgin Group, who focuses on employee satisfaction. Another is Quint Studer, a philanthropist and former healthcare executive who often speaks on workplace culture. A leader or administrator may have a great strategy, but, in the absence of the right culture, expectations are unlikely to be met." /> </Block> </Content> </Document>

<Document xmlns:xsd="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance"> <Content> <Block> <Style FlowDirection="LeftToRight" LeadingMargin="0" TrailingMargin="0" FirstLineMargin="0" Justification="Left" LineSpacingRule="Multiple" LineSpacing="21" SpacingBefore="0" SpacingAfter="8"> <ListStyle ListType="None" ListTypeFormat="Parentheses" Color="#000000" BulletFont="Arial" /> </Style> <Span Text="“Train people well enough so they can leave; treat them well enough so they don’t want to.” -RB
" /> </Block> <Block> <Style FlowDirection="LeftToRight" LeadingMargin="0" TrailingMargin="0" FirstLineMargin="0" Justification="Left" LineSpacingRule="Multiple" LineSpacing="21" SpacingBefore="0" SpacingAfter="8"> <ListStyle ListType="None" ListTypeFormat="Parentheses" Color="#000000" BulletFont="Arial" /> </Style> <Span Text="“Take care of your employees and they will take care of your business. It’s as simple as that.” -RB
" /> </Block> <Block> <Style FlowDirection="LeftToRight" LeadingMargin="0" TrailingMargin="0" FirstLineMargin="0" Justification="Left" LineSpacingRule="Multiple" LineSpacing="21" SpacingBefore="0" SpacingAfter="8"> <ListStyle ListType="None" ListTypeFormat="Parentheses" BulletFont="Arial" /> </Style> <Span Text="“Culture outperforms strategy every time; and culture with strategy is unbeatable.” -QS" /> </Block> </Content> </Document>

<Document xmlns:xsd="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance"> <Content> <Block> <Style /> <Span Text="Take a moment to find and read your employing facility/organization’s mission and vision. Identify a change initiative within your organization that supported that mission or vision (e.g., construction of a new facility, renovation of a unit, etc.). Now consider what type of influence prompted that initiative: was it external, such as patient satisfaction ratings? Was it internal, like the scenario where the nurse reported an error that was addressed, or was it a combination of both?" /> </Block> </Content> </Document>
